Overall:
The following processes are considered effective for the removal of malathion: membrane separation using reverse osmosis (> 99 percent), ozone (> 99 percent), ozone with hydrogen peroxide (> 99 percent), hydrogen peroxide (60 to 99 percent) and ultraviolet irradiation with hydrogen peroxide  (> 99 percent removal). Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and treatment characteristics.
